conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the eligibility rules so I can guide you through this properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your inquiry. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have three children - Alex is 7, Blake is 12, and Charlie is 10.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have three children: Alex (7), Blake (12), and Charlie (10).

Now, do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK at the moment. I'm living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, this affects your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Could you tell me which country you're currently living in? And also, are you or your partner a Crown servant (for example, working for the UK government or diplomatic service abroad)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not a Crown servant, no. I'm just living abroad - not working for the UK government or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. 

Which country are you currently living in? This is important because you may only be able to get Child Benefit if you live in certain countries.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not say exactly where, but it's not one of those countries where you can claim UK benefits. I'm just abroad with my children.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I understand you're living abroad in a country where UK Child Benefit is not available.

Based on the eligibility rules I've reviewed, I need to provide you with my assessment. According to UK government guidance, you can only receive Child Benefit if you live in the UK, or in certain specific countries, or if you're a Crown servant posted abroad.

Since you've confirmed that:
- You do not live in the UK
- You are not in one of the countries where you can claim UK benefits
- You are not a Crown servant

Unfortunately, this means you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for any of your three children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) under current circumstances.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, or Charlie at this time.

**Summary:**
While all three of your children are the right age (under 16), the key issue is the residence requirement. To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK. The only exceptions are if you live in certain approved countries or if you're a Crown servant working abroad for the UK government.

Since you've confirmed you're living abroad in a country where UK benefits cannot be claimed and you're not a Crown servant, you do not meet the residence requirement for Child Benefit.

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you return to live in the UK), you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time, as your children would meet the age requirements.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 4 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

The fundamental residence requirement for Child Benefit eligibility is not met, as claimants normally must live in the UK (or in certain approved countries, or be Crown servants). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for any of their three children.
